[2018-07-13 02:23] cogito3 (Cogito): You weren't aggressive enough. First, you got too many drones--this is a low-econ set as you correctly judged, so you should've stopped at 13 (or possibly even lower). Furthermore, getting Tarsiers midgame was bad; Tarsiers are actually a pretty meh unit once you're over the absorb barrier. Once you committed to starting with Grimbotches (which is the correct play imo), you needed to stick with them to the end. Because supply is a concern, I would've started getting 1 botch and 1 Frostbite (which you never bought for some reason) a turn after about the 4th botch. You could then supplement them with Nivos and Steelsplitters (since you do want the forge eventually for a Wall).
[2018-07-13 02:23] cogito3 (Cogito): (incidentally, it's possible Elyot Animus--DD/A--is the correct line for p1 here, since it's a *really* aggressive set)
[2018-07-13 02:25] flameberger: that makes sense. thanks
[2018-07-13 02:26] shadourow (Rando): I don't think that Grimbotches, as first attackers, were good, considering that the opponent didn't go for a low econ : the first 2 botches didn't get their soak value
[2018-07-13 02:27] flameberger: Tarsiers 1st, then switch to botch?
[2018-07-13 02:28] shadourow (Rando): Indeed.
Your 4 first botches let you kill a drone from your opponent, which is nice, but not enough to justify to skip the 2 first tarsiers
[2018-07-13 02:37] shadourow (Rando): Also, I don't think that your conduit was useful there, as your opponent as enough to defend your Nivo charge and you didn't get good value from those (just a 3 damage threat, which weren't hard to defend and disappeared right after. The Nivo charge from your opponent, for example, was worth 3 threat + 2 damages by chilling you 1 :Lifespan: Grimbotch )

I think A Blastforge would have Been better for one Stillspitter then only wall (obviously, you Stillspitter only if your opponent don't go for botches)
[2018-07-13 02:37] flameberger: yeah, I wasnt too happy with the Nivos
[2018-07-13 02:41] shadourow (Rando): Also, I don't think That Flame got too many drones @Cogito , how many drones you have also depends on how many your opponent have.
As P1, without being attacked, I don't see a line without getting at least the 15 drones he gets
[2018-07-13 02:41] shadourow (Rando): Except Elyot Animus ofc
[2018-07-13 02:42] flameberger: I wasn't planning on going too aggro until I saw his 3rd engi
[2018-07-13 02:42] flameberger: not my usual playstyle
[2018-07-13 02:42] flameberger: though I can see how the set warrants it
[2018-07-13 02:43] shadourow (Rando): Actually, Natural animus (DD/DD/DDA as P1) really isn't aggro
[2018-07-13 02:43] shadourow (Rando): Elyot animus is (DD/A)
[2018-07-13 02:43] shadourow (Rando): Natural animus is just... the classic line
[2018-07-13 02:44] flameberger: right, I mean I wasn't even considering anything other than natural until he got greedy, then it was like, ok how do I punish this?
[2018-07-13 02:47] shadourow (Rando): And you choosed Grimbotches over Tarsiers, ok, in this situation that's not a bad idea indeed (even if I think that something like TT/TGrimbotch then spam grimbotchs is better in the long term)
